claim: Verification confirms: gathered evidence is sufficient, no assumptions presented as facts, all evidence paths are usable with exact repo-relative locations, and no unresolved missing data remains.

evidence.summary: Agent prompt enforces scope separation: WebFetch/WebSearch records must be marked as scope=external and cannot be used as project facts.
evidence.why: Code-level contract prevents external evidence from being treated as project facts.

evidence.summary: Runtime enforcement blocks external-only evidence via PROJECT_EVIDENCE_PATH_REQUIRED and PROJECT_FACT_REQUIRES_PROJECT_EVIDENCE error codes.
evidence.why: Implementation guarantees project facts require project-scoped evidence.

evidence.summary: Test validates external-only evidence blocks PRD readiness with PROJECT_FACT_REQUIRES_PROJECT_EVIDENCE error.
evidence.why: Test coverage proves the enforcement works as specified.

evidence.summary: Test validates evidence records missing scope field are blocked during normalization.
evidence.why: Test coverage proves scope declaration is enforced.

risks: If additional provider execution paths beyond the Claude provider do not enforce scope separation, external research could still be mistaken for project facts.

result.notes: Verification complete. Explorer and cross-checker provided consistent, fact-backed claims with zero assumptions and zero missing data. All evidence records include usable repo-relative paths with line numbers. Evidence agreement shows pass with no conflicts. Acceptance criteria met: agents can use WebFetch/WebSearch as external evidence marked scope=external, and project-scoped path evidence is required for target-project facts.
